sections.section_title: Apply Secure Configurations to All System Components

items.requirement_raw: System components are configured and managed securely. Note: For SAQ A, Requirement 2.2.2 applies to e-commerce merchants’ vendor default accounts on web servers.
items.question: Have you changed or disabled all vendor default passwords, accounts, and insecure default settings?
items.help_text: System components are configured and managed securely. Note: For SAQ A, Requirement 2.2.2 applies to e-commerce merchants’ vendor default accounts on web servers.

items.requirement_raw: Vendor default accounts are managed as follows: If the vendor default account(s) will be used, the default password is changed per Requirement 8.3.6. If the vendor default account(s) will not be used, the account is removed or disabled.
items.question: Have you changed or disabled all vendor default passwords, accounts, and insecure default settings?
items.help_text: Vendor default accounts are managed as follows: If the vendor default account(s) will be used, the default password is changed per Requirement 8.3.6. If the vendor default account(s) will not be used, the account is removed or disabled.

items.requirement_raw: Processes and mechanisms for protecting stored account data are defined and understood.
items.question: Do you have controls in place to ensure that processes and mechanisms for protecting stored account data are defined and understood?
items.help_text: Processes and mechanisms for protecting stored account data are defined and understood.

items.requirement_raw: All security policies and operational procedures that are identified in Requirement 3 are: Documented. Kept up to date. In use. Known to all affected parties.
items.question: Do you have documented, current procedures for Protect Stored Account Data, and do the affected teams know and follow them?
items.help_text: All security policies and operational procedures that are identified in Requirement 3 are: Documented. Kept up to date. In use. Known to all affected parties. SAQ Completion Guidance:
expected_testing_raw: Selection of any of the In Place responses for Requirement 3.1.1 means that, if the merchant has paper storage of account data, the merchant has policies and
procedures in place that govern merchant activities for Requirement 3. This helps to ensure personnel are aware of and following security policies and documented
operational procedures for managing the secure storage of any paper records with account data.
If merchant does not store paper records with account data, mark this requirement as Not Applicable and complete Appendix C: Explanation of Requirements Noted as

items.requirement_raw: Storage of account data is kept to a minimum.
items.question: Do you have controls in place to ensure that storage of account data is kept to a minimum?
items.help_text: Storage of account data is kept to a minimum.

items.requirement_raw: Account data storage is kept to a minimum through implementation of data retention and disposal policies, procedures, and processes that include at least the following: Coverage for all locations of stored account data. Coverage for any sensitive authentication data (SAD) stored prior to completion of authorization. This bullet is a best practice until its effective date; refer to Applicability Notes below for details. Limiting data storage amount and retention time to that which is required for legal or regulatory, and/or business requirements. Specific retention requirements for stored account data that defines length of retention period and includes a documented business justification. Processes for secure deletion or rendering account data unrecoverable when no longer needed per the retention policy. A process for verifying, at least once every three months, that stored account data exceeding the defined retention period has been securely deleted or rendered unrecoverable.
items.question: Do you have a retention and secure deletion process for stored account data?
items.help_text: Account data storage is kept to a minimum through implementation of data retention and disposal policies, procedures, and processes that include at least the following: Coverage for all locations of stored account data. Coverage for any sensitive...

sections.section_title: Develop and Maintain Secure Systems and Software

items.requirement_raw: Security vulnerabilities are identified and addressed.
items.question: Do you have controls in place to ensure that security vulnerabilities are identified and addressed?
items.help_text: Security vulnerabilities are identified and addressed.

items.requirement_raw: Security vulnerabilities are identified and managed as follows: New security vulnerabilities are identified using industry-recognized sources for security vulnerability information, including alerts from international and national computer emergency response teams (CERTs). Vulnerabilities are assigned a risk ranking based on industry best practices and consideration of potential impact. Risk rankings identify, at a minimum, all vulnerabilities considered to be a high-risk or critical to the environment. Bullet intentionally left blank for this SAQ.
items.question: Do you identify, rank, and manage security vulnerabilities based on risk?
items.help_text: Security vulnerabilities are identified and managed as follows: New security vulnerabilities are identified using industry-recognized sources for security vulnerability information, including alerts from international and national computer emergency...

items.requirement_raw: All system components are protected from known vulnerabilities by installing applicable security patches/updates as follows: Patches/updates for critical vulnerabilities (identified according to the risk ranking processes at Requirement 6.3.1) are installed within one month of release. Bullet intentionally left blank for this SAQ.
items.question: Do you install security patches and updates within the required timeframes?
items.help_text: All system components are protected from known vulnerabilities by installing applicable security patches/updates as follows: Patches/updates for critical vulnerabilities (identified according to the risk ranking processes at Requirement 6.3.1) are...
expected_testing_raw: installed to recent vendor patch lists.

sections.section_title: Identify Users and Authenticate Access to System Components

items.requirement_raw: User identification and related accounts for users and administrators are strictly managed throughout an account’s lifecycle.
items.question: Do you have controls in place to ensure that user identification and related accounts for users and administrators are strictly managed throughout an account’s lifecycle?
items.help_text: User identification and related accounts for users and administrators are strictly managed throughout an account’s lifecycle.

items.requirement_raw: All users are assigned a unique ID before access to system components or cardholder data is allowed.
items.question: Does every user have a unique ID or account for access to in-scope systems?
items.help_text: All users are assigned a unique ID before access to system components or cardholder data is allowed. This requirement is not intended to apply to user accounts within point-of-sale terminals that have access to only one card number at a time to facilitate...

items.requirement_raw: Group, shared, or generic IDs, or other shared authentication credentials are only used when necessary on an exception basis, and are managed as follows: ID use is prevented unless needed for an exceptional circumstance. Use is limited to the time needed for the exceptional circumstance. Business justification for use is documented. Use is explicitly approved by management. Individual user identity is confirmed before access to an account is granted. Every action taken is attributable to an individual user.
items.question: Do you have controls in place to ensure that group, shared, or generic IDs, or other shared authentication credentials are only used when necessary on an exception basis, and are managed as follows: ID use is prevented unless needed for an exceptional circumstance. items.requirement_raw: Access for terminated users is immediately revoked. • Examine information sources for terminated users.
items.question: Do you have controls in place to ensure that access for terminated users is immediately revoked. • Examine information sources for terminated users?
items.help_text: Access for terminated users is immediately revoked. • Examine information sources for terminated users.

items.requirement_raw: Strong authentication for users and administrators is established and managed.

items.help_text: Strong authentication for users and administrators is established and managed.

items.requirement_raw: All user access to system components for users and administrators is authenticated via at least one of the following authentication factors: Something you know, such as a password or passphrase. Something you have, such as a token device or smart card. Something you are, such as a biometric element.

items.requirement_raw: If passwords/passphrases are used as authentication factors to meet Requirement 8.3.1, they are set and reset for each user as follows: Set to a unique value for first-time use and upon reset. Forced to be changed immediately after the first use.

items.requirement_raw: If passwords/passphrases are used as authentication factors to meet Requirement 8.3.1, they meet the following minimum level of complexity: A minimum length of 12 characters (or IF the system does not support 12 characters, a minimum length of eight characters). Contain both numeric and alphabetic characters.

items.requirement_raw: Individuals are not allowed to submit a new password/passphrase that is the same as any of the last four passwords/passphrases used.

sections.section_title: Restrict Physical Access to Cardholder Data

items.requirement_raw: Media with cardholder data is securely stored, accessed, distributed, and destroyed. Note: For SAQ A, Requirements at 9.4 only apply to merchants with paper records (for example, receipts or printed reports) with account data, including primary account numbers (PANs).

items.requirement_raw: All media with cardholder data is physically secured. • Examine documentation.

items.help_text: All media with cardholder data is physically secured. • Examine documentation.

items.help_text: Offline media backups with cardholder data are stored in a secure location.

items.requirement_raw: Hard-copy materials with cardholder data are destroyed when no longer needed for business or legal reasons, as follows: Materials are cross-cut shredded, incinerated, or pulped so that cardholder data cannot be reconstructed. Materials are stored in secure storage containers prior to destruction.

sections.section_title: Test Security of Systems and Networks Regularly

items.requirement_raw: External and internal vulnerabilities are regularly identified, prioritized, and addressed.
items.question: Do you have controls in place to ensure that external and internal vulnerabilities are regularly identified, prioritized, and addressed?
items.help_text: External and internal vulnerabilities are regularly identified, prioritized, and addressed.

items.requirement_raw: External vulnerability scans are performed as follows: At least once every three months. By PCI SSC Approved Scanning Vendor (ASV). Vulnerabilities are resolved and ASV Program Guide requirements for a passing scan are met. Rescans are performed as needed to confirm that vulnerabilities are resolved per the ASV Program Guide requirements for a passing scan.
items.question: Do you perform required vulnerability scans and address any findings?
items.help_text: External vulnerability scans are performed as follows: At least once every three months. By PCI SSC Approved Scanning Vendor (ASV). Vulnerabilities are resolved and ASV Program Guide requirements for a passing scan are met. Rescans are performed as needed...

items.requirement_raw: External vulnerability scans are performed after any significant change as follows: Vulnerabilities that are scored 4.0 or higher by the CVSS are resolved. Rescans are conducted as needed. Scans are performed by qualified personnel and organizational independence of the tester exists (not required to be a QSA or ASV).
items.question: Do you perform required vulnerability scans and address any findings?
items.help_text: External vulnerability scans are performed after any significant change as follows: Vulnerabilities that are scored 4.0 or higher by the CVSS are resolved. Rescans are conducted as needed. Scans are performed by qualified personnel and organizational...

sections.section_title: Support Information Security with Organizational Policies and Programs

items.requirement_raw: Risk to information assets associated with third-party service provider (TPSP) relationships is managed.
items.question: Do you manage third-party service providers with clear oversight, agreements, and responsibility tracking?
items.help_text: Risk to information assets associated with third-party service provider (TPSP) relationships is managed.

items.requirement_raw: A list of all third-party service providers (TPSPs) with which account data is shared or that could affect the security of account data is maintained, including a description for each of the services provided.
items.question: Do you manage third-party service providers with clear oversight, agreements, and responsibility tracking?
items.help_text: A list of all third-party service providers (TPSPs) with which account data is shared or that could affect the security of account data is maintained, including a description for each of the services provided.

items.requirement_raw: Written agreements with TPSPs are maintained as follows: Written agreements are maintained with all TPSPs with which account data is shared or that could affect the security of the CDE. Written agreements include acknowledgments from TPSPs that TPSPs are responsible for the security of account data the TPSPs possess or otherwise store, process, or transmit on behalf of the entity, or to the extent that the TPSP could impact the security of the entity’s cardholder data and/or sensitive authentication data.
items.question: Do you manage third-party service providers with clear oversight, agreements, and responsibility tracking?
items.help_text: Written agreements with TPSPs are maintained as follows: Written agreements are maintained with all TPSPs with which account data is shared or that could affect the security of the CDE. Written agreements include acknowledgments from TPSPs that TPSPs are...

items.requirement_raw: An established process is implemented for engaging TPSPs, including proper due diligence prior to engagement.
items.question: Do you manage third-party service providers with clear oversight, agreements, and responsibility tracking?
items.help_text: An established process is implemented for engaging TPSPs, including proper due diligence prior to engagement.

items.requirement_raw: A program is implemented to monitor TPSPs’ PCI DSS compliance status at least once every 12 months.
items.question: Do you manage third-party service providers with clear oversight, agreements, and responsibility tracking?
items.help_text: A program is implemented to monitor TPSPs’ PCI DSS compliance status at least once every 12 months.

items.requirement_raw: Information is maintained about which PCI DSS requirements are managed by each TPSP, which are managed by the entity, and any that are shared between the TPSP and the entity.
items.question: Do you manage third-party service providers with clear oversight, agreements, and responsibility tracking?
items.help_text: Information is maintained about which PCI DSS requirements are managed by each TPSP, which are managed by the entity, and any that are shared between the TPSP and the entity. SAQ Completion Guidance:
expected_testing_raw: Selection of any of the In Place responses for requirements at 12.8.1 through 12.8.5 means that the merchant has a list of, and agreements with, service providers
they share account data with or that could impact the security of the merchant’s cardholder data environment. For example, such agreements would be applicable if a
merchant uses a document-retention company to store paper documents that include account data or if a merchant’s vendor accesses merchant systems remotely to

items.requirement_raw: Suspected and confirmed security incidents that could impact the CDE are responded to immediately.
items.question: Do you have controls in place to ensure that suspected and confirmed security incidents that could impact the CDE are responded to immediately?
items.help_text: Suspected and confirmed security incidents that could impact the CDE are responded to immediately.

items.requirement_raw: An incident response plan exists and is ready to be activated in the event of a suspected or confirmed security incident. The plan includes, but is not limited to: Roles, responsibilities, and communication and contact strategies in the event of a suspected or confirmed security incident, including notification of payment brands and acquirers, at a minimum. Incident response procedures with specific containment and mitigation activities for different types of incidents. Business recovery and continuity procedures. Data backup processes. Analysis of legal requirements for reporting compromises. Coverage and responses of all critical system components. Reference or inclusion of incident response procedures from the payment brands.
items.question: Do you have a documented incident response plan that is current and ready to use?
items.help_text: An incident response plan exists and is ready to be activated in the event of a suspected or confirmed security incident. The plan includes, but is not limited to: Roles, responsibilities, and communication and contact strategies in the event of a...
expected_testing_raw: Selection of any of the In Place responses for Requirement 12.10.1 means that the merchant has documented an incident response and escalation plan to be used for
emergencies, consistent with the size and complexity of the merchant’s operations. For example, such a plan could be a simple document posted in the back office
that lists who to call in the event of various situations with an annual review to confirm it is still accurate, but could extend all the way to a full incident response plan
including backup “hotsite” facilities and thorough annual testing. This plan should be readily available to all personnel as a resource in an emergency.
